rationale: In the citing paper, Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) is cited only in the introduction as prior evidence that hedonic versus utilitarian value relates to outcomes such as price premium and ownership duration. The citation serves as broad motivation for why measuring hedonic/utilitarian attitudes might matter managerially. The paper does not build its scale-development logic, hypotheses, methods, or validation tests on Dhar and Wertenbroch's acquisition-versus-forfeiture framework, so the role is a passing background reference.
